summary: Comprehensive development-process blueprint with strong self-description and local recoverability, but undermined by substantial redundancy between enforces() and when/then chains and by mixing product-ideal policy with machine-specific operational detail that belongs in an implementation-resolved layer.
strengths: Clear role declaration and thorough defines block make the file fully self-describing without external context
Complete lifecycle coverage from feature-branch creation through post-deploy verification with explicit causal ordering between phases
Causal chains between blueprint review, commit, implementation, and rollout are unambiguous and well-sequenced
Explicit handling of both operator and provider-agent actors ensures the process applies uniformly
Locally recoverable: a new reader can reconstruct the entire workflow from this file plus the language root alone
weaknesses: Heavy redundancy: most enforces() rules are restated in when/then chains, inflating token count without adding semantic content

Abstraction layer bleed: exact CLI commands (git worktree add -b ..., bun run deploy-manager-runtime), machine-specific paths (~/workspace/projects-worktrees/, /home/ec2-user/workspace/), and tool names (agent-browser) are implementation-resolved concerns embedded in a product ideal
The provider-agent when/then block uses long inline prose strings instead of defined concepts, reducing density and increasing interpretive slack compared to the rest of the file
Several defines() entries restate enforces() content in slightly different words rather than adding new semantic value
